methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rainbow Robotics, a South Korean robotics and manufacturing company founded by Oh Jun-ho, was acquired after raising $50M+ in total capital. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers within Robotics & Manufacturing, including one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private competitors such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to this analysis. The target's stage and valuation tier did not yield a sufficiently narrow peer subset—zero same-stage comparables, zero adjacent-stage comparables, and zero similar-tier private companies were identified. A full-sector median approach would introduce excessive noise and render results unreliable given the heterogeneity of the robotics market. The single public comparable and five identified private peers provide limited datapoints for robust triangulation.

Without sufficient narrow-set peers to support quantitative multiples-based analysis, this valuation relies on constraints rather than direct comparable ranges. The $50M+ raised capital floor anchors the lower bound, while the public and private peer universe suggests the acquisition price likely reflected a revenue multiple or ARR multiple within range of applied robotics and manufacturing automation deals, though specific transaction data is not available. Key uncertainties include the target's revenue run rate at exit, the acquirer's strategic premium, and the precise stage of maturity at acquisition.
